title: In Situ Hybridization of Brain Slices.
author_string: Kanayama, Tomohiro, Tomita, Hiroyuki, Hara, Akira
abstract: In situ hybridization (ISH) is an important technique for identifying gene expression at the cellular level in various organs, including brain slices. This approach hybridizes nucleic acid probes to cellular mRNA, allowing the detection of transcriptional products. Recent advances have enabled RNA preservation in formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) samples, making ISH applicable to brain tumor diagnosis and research. Here, we provide a concise overview of the standard application of chromogenic ISH in neuroscience research and neuropathology practice using FFPE blocks of brain slice sections.
